judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with accurate citations; they are comparable, with Report 1 and Report 2 each adding extra valid findings (UI mismatch, missing tests, null-settings crash risk).

report_1.evidence: Reminders **are sent automatically** — the clinic is silently opted in. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... line 11 checks whether the key is missing... line 12 returns `True` in that case.

report_2.evidence: Yes, reminders **are sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)`... the `is None` branch at line 11 is taken, and line 12 returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` to invert the default so missing/null means **disabled**

report_2.evidence: Where I'd change it: `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` — change `return True` to `return False` inside `auto_send_reminders_enabled`

report_1.evidence: `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (the resolver this was copied from) gates a purely cosmetic UI preference — grandfathering existing tenants to "on" is low-risk and reversible... `auto_send_reminders_enabled` is a different risk category entirely: it triggers unsolicited, automated outbound SMS and email

report_2.evidence: structurally identical to `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... Defaulting a calendar-density UI preference to "on" is low-stakes and fully reversible. Defaulting an automated SMS/email-to-patients feature to "on" is not

report_1.evidence: Add an env-level master switch AND'd with the per-tenant value. Right now `reminders.py`'s `run()` has no global gate at all... Without one, there's no way to instantly halt reminders for all tenants at once

report_2.evidence: There is no rollout or kill-switch mechanism anywhere in this repo... no global environment gate... I'd want at least a global kill switch independent of per-tenant settings
